data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Microsoft employs a microservices architecture to ensure scalability and flexibility across its platforms.
data.technical_spec: Services are decoupled and independently deployable components.

data.title: Cloud-Native Infrastructure
data.description: Leverages Azure cloud computing services to support high traffic and storage needs.
data.technical_spec: Built on Azure, utilizing cloud-native principles such as scalability, elasticity, and message-driven services.

data.title: API-Driven Development
data.description: APIs are central to Microsoft's web service design enabling integration and interaction across ecosystems.
data.technical_spec: RESTful API standards with OAuth2 for authentication.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: Supports real-time processing and response capabilities across platforms.
data.technical_spec: Based on Azure Event Grid and Azure Functions for event handling.

data.title: Containerization
data.description: Use of containers to streamline deployment and development environments.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Kubernetes and Docker for efficient container orchestration.
